title: More Nonsense Limerick 31
content:
There was an old person of Hove,
    Who frequented the depths of a grove;
    Where he studied his books, 
    With the wrens and the rooks,
    That tranquil old person of Hove.
meaning:
"More Nonsense Limerick 31" is a quintessential example of Edward Lear's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
